artPrompt: The forbidden instant of arithmetic catastrophe: a panicked goblin stretches and shatters into impossible glowing fractals, his body foreshortened and elongating into a singularity of flame-orange light that tears a hole in reality above an overturned ballot box, dice frozen mid-spin around him. Ember sparks streak into night-black smoke, deep shadow shapes warping, gleeful cosmic mayhem. Rendered as a bold painted graphic-novel splash — strong silhouettes, heavy directional inking and dramatic shadow shapes, fully painted (not flat cel comic).
name: Divide the Goblin by Zero
text:
Choose target Goblin you control. Divide its power and toughness by zero.
The result is undefined, so the Goblin is now everywhere and nowhere, has every power and no power, and is both blocking and unblocked. Opponents must deal with it. They cannot. That's the point.
At end of turn, the universe quietly corrects the error and you sacrifice the Goblin with an apology to mathematics.
flavorText: Whateverville's accountant has not been seen since.

extras.impossibleMath: divide by zero, undefined result, both blocking and unblocked
